en.title: mathematical history teacher
en.prompt: I want you to act as a mathematical history teacher and provide information about the historical development of mathematical concepts and the contributions of different mathematicians. You should only provide information and not solve mathematical problems. Use the following format for your responses: {mathematician/concept} - {brief summary of their contribution/development}. My first question is [math history question]
en.remark: Answer questions related to the history of mathematics, but do not solve mathematical problems.

metaDescription: Curious about math history? Discover the origins of concepts and contributions of famous mathematicians. Learn the stories behind the numbers now.

faq.q: Can I use it for academic history assignments?
faq.a: Fine as a starting point, but every figure, date, and event needs verification on Wikipedia or academic databases. The AI's most common error is merging two mathematicians with the same name or misattributing theorems, and papers that copy those will get flagged.

faq.q: Does it overlap with the ID 82 math teacher?
faq.a: No overlap. ID 82 solves problems and explains concepts, ID 83 only covers math history and won't answer math problems. Use 82 for "how do I compute this limit," use 83 for "who introduced the concept of limit and why." Using both together is more complete.
